steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail shortcut. The Settings app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings | Step 3: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
The goal is to turn on Wi-Fi, which requires accessing the system settings. Since the Settings app is not present on the current home screen shortcuts, I need to launch it. I will use a system-level open command to launch the Settings app directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the main page of the "Settings" app. Visible options include "Search settings", "Network & internet" (with subtext "Mobile, Wi-Fi, hotspot"), "Connected devices", "Apps", "Notifications", "Battery", and others.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings → | Step 3: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
According to the current plan, the next step is to navigate to the network settings where Wi-Fi controls are located. The "Network & internet" option is clearly visible at the top of the list and explicitly mentions "Wi-Fi" in its description. I will tap on this option to proceed.

[Memory]
Settings app successfully opened. Next target is the Wi-Fi toggle within the Network & internet menu.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Network & internet" settings page. Visible options include "Internet" (subtext: Networks available), "Calls & SMS", "SIMs", "Airplane mode" (currently off), "Hotspot & tethering", "Data Saver", and "VPN". There is no direct Wi-Fi toggle switch on this screen; instead, Wi-Fi settings are grouped under the "Internet" option.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed there would be a direct Wi-Fi toggle on this screen (Step 3: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle). However, modern Android versions group Wi-Fi under the "Internet" menu. I need to adjust the plan to enter this menu first.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings ✓ | Step 3: Tap on "Internet" to access Wi-Fi settings → | Step 4: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the updated plan, I need to access the Wi-Fi settings which are located inside the "Internet" menu. The "Internet" option is at the top of the list, indicating it handles available networks including Wi-Fi. I will tap on "Internet" to proceed.

[Memory]
Navigated to Network & internet. Wi-Fi controls are not on the main network page but are nested under the "Internet" menu. Next target is finding the Wi-Fi toggle inside the "Internet" page.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The "Wi-Fi" section is visible, and the toggle switch next to it is in the ON position (blue, switched to the right). Below it, "AndroidWifi" is listed as a saved network, and "T-Mobile" is currently connected on the 3G network.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings ✓ | Step 3: Tap on "Internet" to access Wi-Fi settings ✓ | Step 4: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete ✓

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Wi-Fi toggle is already in the ON position. The goal of the task "Turn wifi on" has been achieved. There is no further action required. I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Navigated to "Internet" settings. Wi-Fi toggle is confirmed to be ON. Task objective met.
